summary: Large gm_16F1 decompilation PR for post-match decision/bonus logic. It replaced several gm_16F1 stubs with C implementations, added typed prototypes and struct fields needed by the code, corrected a gm_16AE callsite for fn_80171DC4, and tuned gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 to match. decomp-dev reported +2080 matched bytes, five new 100% matches, and major partial matches for remaining large gm_16F1 functions; there was no PR body or human review text captured.

key_files.role: Main implementation work: fn_8016F39C, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, fn_8016FAD4, fn_8016FFD4, fn_801701C0, fn_80171BA4, fn_80171DC4, plus fn_8016F870/fn_80170110 flag-lookup cleanup.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed prototypes and corrected fn_8016F1F0 and fn_80171DC4 signatures.
Added static sdata-like labels and lbl_803B7A60_t zero/threshold tables used by the new ranking/special-decision code.
Expanded lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t layout with x7 and x58 subfields xA and x40 while preserving offset padding.
Split pl_804D6470_t padding to expose x13C and x140 thresholds used by fn_801701C0.
Adjusted fn_8017F1B8 mask/flag temporary to reach a 100% match per bot report.
Updated fn_8016C7D0 to call no-argument fn_80171DC4().

what_changed: Implemented gm_16F1 decision list/search/count helpers: fn_8016F39C fills HSD_Text entries with item IDs, fn_8016F548 searches forward then wraps backward, fn_8016F740 searches after the current entry, and fn_8016F9A8 counts matching entries.

Implemented scoring/evaluation functions fn_8016FAD4 and fn_8016FFD4. They use lbl_803D5A4C metadata, lbl_803D5648 score values, per-player x58 stats, rankings, masks, and pl_80039418/fn_801701C0 predicates.

Implemented large special-decision predicate fn_801701C0 for kinds 0xD7 through 0xFF, using rules->pad3F0 flags, x58 per-player results, Player/pl stat APIs, small sorted arrays, rankings, and self-calls to exclude stronger related awards.

Implemented fn_80171BA4 to compute six-slot player scores/rankings from KOs, falls, suicides, team membership, and match suicide multiplier; implemented fn_80171DC4 to set or clear winner/loser tracking flags in lbl_8046DBC8 based on lbl_804D65A8 and lbl_804D65B0.
Simplified repeated lbl_803D5A4C flag-table scans in fn_8016F870 and fn_80170110 by using fn_8016F180 for the decision flags.

Corrected public prototypes: fn_8016F1F0 now returns s32 instead of short; several gm_16F1 functions now have typed parameter lists; fn_80171DC4 is void(void), causing gm_16AE.c to drop an unused HSD_GObj argument.
Added structure fields needed by the implementation: lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.x7, x58[].xA, x58[].x40, and pl_804D6470_t.x13C/x140.
Adjusted gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 by storing the u8 fn_8016F180 result in a local output variable and checking output & mask; decomp-dev reported this item improved from 97.73% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: The code consistently splits ordinary decision kinds below 0xD7 from special kinds at or above 0xD7, routing the former through pl_80039418 and the latter through fn_801701C0. This pattern is reused across list, search, count, and scoring functions.
Replacing manual sentinel scans of lbl_803D5A4C with fn_8016F180 reduced duplicated control flow and made the intended table flag lookup explicit.
Prototype fixes were applied with their callsites: fn_80171DC4 became a no-argument function and gm_16AE.c was updated in the same PR, avoiding a mismatched callback-style signature.
The struct/header changes expose only the offsets proven necessary by the decomp while preserving anonymous padding, which is safer than inventing semantic names too early.
The implementation uses matching-friendly local arrays, explicit u8/u16/s32 casts, pointer induction variables, and PAD_STACK markers where the diff needed stack-shape control.

Static initializer labels and the lbl_803B7A60 zero/threshold table were introduced in gm_16F1.static.h so stack arrays in fn_8016FAD4/fn_801701C0 can be initialized in a way that resembles the original code.
decomp_lessons: When a gm result function repeatedly checks decision-table flags, first identify the helper that returns the table field; reusing it can remove brittle duplicate sentinel loops.
For callback-looking functions, do not assume the HSD_GObj argument is consumed. fn_80171DC4 matched as void(void), even though it is called from fn_8016C7D0(HSD_GObj*).
Complex result/bonus functions may require filling adjacent struct offsets in both gm and pl type headers before the C can be expressed cleanly.
Rank/score code in this area uses six player slots but many decision predicates iterate four active result entries; preserve those loop bounds as observed.
Small local ranking arrays are sensitive to initialization width and signedness. The PR copied sdata labels into u8 arrays and used explicit casts to steer MWCC output.
Bot reports should be checked for collateral regressions: this PR improved gm_16F1 heavily but also reported a small unmatched-item regression in gmresult fn_80174468.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved doldecomp placeholder naming for unknown functions and labels, such as fn_801701C0, lbl_803D5A4C, lbl_8046DBC8, and lbl_804DA2F0.
New struct fields use offset-style names like x7, xA, x40, x13C, and x140, with pad fields split to maintain layout.
Header prototypes retain address comments such as /* 16F39C */ and use concrete primitive/HSD types once signatures are known.
No semantic renames were introduced for the newly implemented functions; the evidence is decomp/matching progress rather than naming cleanup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp-dev reported five new 100% matches: gm_16F1 fn_80171DC4, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, and gmregclear fn_8017F1B8.
decomp-dev reported large partial improvements for gm_16F1: fn_801701C0 to 91.77%, fn_8016FAD4 to 95.15%, fn_80171BA4 to 94.43%, fn_8016F39C to 92.99%, and fn_8016FFD4 to 99.30%.

The squash commit notes mention remaining diffs such as register allocation/scheduling cosmetics, stack frame size, sdata2 relocations, mullw operand order, and dead code eliminated by MWCC; these are commit-message evidence rather than review comments.
Several functions end with PAD_STACK even after return, indicating stack-frame padding was used as a deliberate matching tactic.
fn_8017F1B8 used a temporary output variable and operand order output & mask to obtain the final gmregclear match.
fn_801701C0 encodes related award exclusions with self-calls, such as checking 0xD9/0xDA before 0xD7/0xD8 and checking 0xDB/0xDD/0xDF/0xE1 before weaker related cases.

review_feedback.detail: PR body was empty/null and no human review comments or reviews were captured in the slice.
Matched code became 63.80% (+0.05%, +2080 bytes) for GALE01.
One reported regression: main/melee/gm/gmresult fn_80174468 decreased by 4 bytes, from 89.84% to 89.49%.

follow_up_queries: How did PR 2411 implement gm_16F1 fn_801701C0 special decision kinds 0xD7 through 0xFF?
Which gm/types.h and pl/types.h offsets were exposed for gm_16F1 result matching?
Why is fn_80171DC4 declared void(void) and called without HSD_GObj from gm_16AE fn_8016C7D0?
What matching tactics were used for gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 output & mask?
What caused or later fixed the gmresult fn_80174468 regression reported alongside PR 2411?
